title: Bankruptcy proceedings commenced
description: Tokyo District Court commenced bankruptcy proceedings for MtGox Co., Ltd. on April 24, 2014.

title: Chapter 11 filing and collapse
description: FTX and affiliated debtors filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection on November 11, 2022.

title: Binance launched
description: Binance began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: CCAA protection and insolvency crisis
description: Quadriga sought and received CCAA protection on February 5, 2019 after the platform had shut down on January 28, 2019.

title: FinCEN penalties and U.S. enforcement action
description: FinCEN assessed penalties against BTC-e on July 26, 2017 as U.S. enforcement action escalated.

title: January 2019 exchange hack
description: Official liquidation materials describe a serious January 2019 hack that caused severe operational and reputational damage.
notes: The official source confirms the hack occurred in January 2019, but not the precise day.

title: Liquidators appointed and trading suspended
description: Cryptopia reached an operational end state associated with hack.

title: Operations wound down
description: Global exchange brand from the broader Bittrex lineage, later wound down after regulatory pressure and liquidation proceedings.

title: Operations ceased
description: Centralized exchange founded in 2018 that ceased CEX operations in May 2023 after deteriorating operating conditions and earlier disruption tied to an investigation.

title: Ownership changed through acquisition
description: Poloniex underwent an acquisition-related ownership change.
notes: Ownership transition only; exchange continued operating.

title: Vaultoro launched
description: Vaultoro began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Platform migration underway
description: Official messaging described a temporary migration with customer assets stated to remain safe.
notes: Official site showed a migration/update notice in April 2026 with customer assets stated to remain safe. Launch date uses the May 2015 public launch reference for the trading service; company/about material also claims operations since 2014.

title: Cryptsy launched
description: Cryptsy began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

title: Insolvency crisis became definitive
description: Cryptsy reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Later reporting places the exchange launch in May 2013. The operational end marker uses the January 2016 disclosure of hidden losses and withdrawal failure.

title: BitGrail launched
description: BitGrail began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to year level.

title: Operations ceased
description: BitGrail ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Founding year is treated as 2017 from retrospective reporting. Death marker uses the Florence court order to cease operations on 2018-05-03; the primary collapse trigger remained the February 2018 Nano loss.

title: Operations ceased
description: Livecoin ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Launch uses the specific April 2015 operating-since statement from the shutdown coverage. Some secondary profiles cite earlier founding dates; these are left in notes rather than used as the operational launch marker.

title: Operations wound down
description: CoinExchange.io reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Public reporting indicates trading and deposits halted in October 2019, with withdrawals reportedly remaining available into December 2019.

title: Operations ceased
description: MapleChange ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Public reporting conflicts on whether the terminal crisis is better characterized as a hack or an exit scam.

title: Cobinhood launched
description: Cobinhood began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Operations wound down
description: Cobinhood reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Reuters announced the platform launch in September 2017, but the live platform launch reference used here is 2017-12-18. Shutdown marker uses the 2020-01-10 customer notice.

title: Altsbit launched
description: Altsbit began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

title: Operations ceased
description: Altsbit ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Public coverage indicates the venue was only a few months old by February 2020. The operational end marker uses the published refund deadline in May 2020.

title: Operations wound down
description: WEX reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Launch uses the September 2017 rebrand/launch announcement. Death marker uses the late-November 2018 domain-loss point as an operational end marker; exact final shutdown timing remains contested.

title: Operations wound down
description: BX Thailand reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.

title: Operations interrupted
description: COSS went offline and then entered an acquisition/relaunch process, leaving long-term status ambiguous.
notes: COSS was taken offline on 2020-01-07 and later described acquisition negotiations and a relaunch plan. Because the record is too ambiguous for a clean dead-side classification, it is moved to inactive pending deeper verification.

title: Vircurex launched
description: Vircurex began operating as a centralized exchange; launch date precision is currently limited to month level.

title: Insolvency crisis became definitive
description: Vircurex reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Launch uses repeated October 2011 references. Operational end marker uses the March 2014 withdrawal freeze and account-freeze period, which followed earlier hacks and a reserve crisis.

title: Operations ceased
description: CoinsMarkets ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Launch remains approximate because strong primary founding records are still missing. Operational end marker uses the point when users widely reported trading and withdrawals disabled on 2018-01-05.

title: MintPal launched
description: MintPal began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Operations ceased
description: MintPal ceased operations after the crisis that ultimately ended the platform.
notes: Launch uses a specific February 2014 launch reference. Death marker uses 2014-10-14 when Moolah announced MintPal had been shut down while preparing bankruptcy; later fallout continued after that date.

title: Vault of Satoshi launched
description: Vault of Satoshi began operating as a centralized exchange.

title: Operations wound down
description: Vault of Satoshi reached its terminal operating state on the date used by HEI as the end marker.
notes: Launch uses October 2013 reporting on the exchange going live. Closure date and reason come from the January 2015 shutdown announcement specifying a voluntary end rather than insolvency or theft.

title: Poloniex spins out from Circle
description: Poloniex leadership spun the exchange out of Circle into Polo Digital Assets, Ltd., continuing operations outside Circle.
notes: Ownership / corporate structure change; not a death-side terminal event.

title: Trading and wallet use suspended
description: LocalBitcoins suspended trading and normal wallet use on 2023-02-16, leaving withdrawals only afterward.
notes: Closure was announced on 2023-02-09; this event uses the effective service-stop date.

title: Kraken acquisition announced
description: Kraken announced the Coinsetter acquisition on 2016-01-19 and stated that client accounts would be transferred on 2016-01-26.
notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-01-26 transfer date.

title: Kraken acquisition announced
description: Kraken announced the Cavirtex acquisition on 2016-01-19 and stated that client accounts would be transferred on 2016-01-26.
notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-01-26 transfer date.

title: BitBay rebranded to Zonda
description: BitBay announced on 2021-11-08 that the exchange brand had changed to Zonda, with legacy BitBay platform URLs later redirecting to Zonda domains.
notes: Further downstream rebranding to zondacrypto should be handled as successor lineage, not silent overwrite.

title: BITFRONT service terminated
description: BITFRONT's closure notice wound down service in stages and ended withdrawals and remaining customer access on 2023-03-31.
notes: The initial shutdown announcement was published on 2022-11-28.

title: OPNX ceased operations
description: OPNX told users to withdraw funds and export historical data by 2024-02-14 as part of its February 2024 shutdown.
notes: Shutdown date is supported by strong reporting; launch lineage traces to the March 2023 CoinFLEX-to-OPNX rebrand.

title: Youbit halted trading and filed for bankruptcy
description: Youbit stopped trading and entered bankruptcy proceedings after disclosing a second 2017 hack that reduced customer balances.
notes: The retained marker combines the service halt and bankruptcy filing date used in same-day reporting.

title: Gatecoin ordered to wind up and cease operations
description: Gatecoin ceased operations after a Hong Kong court granted a winding-up order against the company.
notes: Liquidators were formally appointed on 2019-03-20; the event date here follows the operational stop marker.

title: Koinex permanently disabled trading
description: Koinex permanently disabled digital-asset trading on all platforms on 2019-06-27 and moved to withdrawals-only wind-down.
notes: The later withdrawal deadline of 2019-07-15 is kept as supporting context, not the entity death marker.

title: CoinCorner acquired Coinfloor customer base and domains
description: CoinCorner acquired Coinfloor's customer base and domains in October 2021 and began migrating or closing legacy Coinfloor accounts.
notes: Further lineage work should attach the successor relationship once CoinCorner is modeled in canonical data.

title: Kraken acquisition announced
description: Kraken announced the CleverCoin acquisition on 2016-06-28 and said customer accounts would be automatically transferred the next day.
notes: Entity death_date is tracked separately as the 2016-06-29 transfer completion date.

title: Coinnest shutdown process began
description: Coinnest posted a closure notice saying it was no longer operating as of Tuesday and set an April 30 deadline for withdrawals.
notes: The retained event date follows the effective service-stop marker referenced in reporting on April 18–19, 2019.

title: Liqui announced shutdown and 30 day withdrawal window
description: Liqui announced it was shutting down on 2019-01-28 and told users they had 30 days to withdraw funds.
notes: Shutdown rationale quoted in reporting focused on insufficient liquidity and lack of economic justification for continued service.

title: Nova ceased trading and API access
description: Nova said trading would cease and the API would be deactivated on 2019-10-07 as part of its shutdown schedule.
notes: Withdrawals were left open until 2019-10-25, but the operational end marker used here is the trading/API stop date.

title: Interim receiver secured Einstein Exchange
description: The Supreme Court of British Columbia appointed an interim receiver on 2019-11-01, which then secured the premises and shut down platform access.
notes: BCSC said platform counsel had told regulators on 2019-10-31 that the business planned to shut down within 30 to 60 days due to a lack of profit.

title: The Rock Trading interrupted platform operations
description: The Rock Trading said it had to interrupt operation of its platform because of difficulties encountered in liquidity management.
notes: The operational stop preceded judicial liquidation and insolvency proceedings in Milan.

title: VirWoX permanently closed its service
description: VirWoX notified users in late 2019 that the service would permanently close on 2020-01-06 and account access would end that day.
notes: The retained date follows the stated end-of-service date rather than the earlier announcement date.

title: Bitcurex went offline after reported external interference
description: Bitcurex reported asset losses and went offline in October 2016.
notes: Followed by a prosecutor investigation in 2017.

title: Tradehill halted trading again
description: Tradehill halted trading in August 2013 because of banking and regulatory issues.
notes: Earlier 2012 shutdown remains contextual evidence.

title: Coin.mx operators were arrested and charged
description: U.S. authorities charged Coin.mx operators with running an unlawful bitcoin exchange and money-laundering scheme.
notes: Enforcement-side operational end marker.

title: Intersango closed its remaining GBP market
description: Intersango progressively shut markets during 2012 and closed its remaining GBP market in December.
notes: Uses the final GBP closure date.

title: Binance Uganda fully shut down
description: Binance Uganda announced a phased wind-down and fully shut down on 2020-11-28.
notes: Regional Binance surface.

title: Mt. Gox acquired Bitomat
description: After Bitomat lost wallet data and customer balances, Mt. Gox acquired the service and migrated users in August 2011.
notes: Successor is Mt. Gox.

title: Cointrader closed after alleged hack
description: Cointrader said it had closed its doors effective immediately after a debilitating hack.
notes: Immediate shutdown notice.

title: Yunbi announced closure of trading operations
description: Yunbi said it would close trading during the 2017 China crackdown on exchanges.
notes: China crackdown closure.

title: Binance Jersey fully shut down
description: Binance Jersey wound down through November 2020 and fully shut down on 2020-11-30.
notes: Regional Binance surface.

title: Binance Singapore ended DPT services
description: Binance.sg shut down by 2022-02-13 after the operator withdrew its licensing effort.
notes: Regional Binance surface.

title: ACH and wire transfers halted
description: CampBX halted ACH and wire transfers after its bank decided not to work with bitcoin companies because of regulatory uncertainty.
notes: Earlier operational stress marker, not the final terminal event.

title: Georgia cease and desist became final
description: The Georgia Department of Banking and Finance said an order to cease and desist against Bulbul Investments LLC doing business as CampBX became final on 2018-07-25.
notes: Used as the terminal marker for public-ready handling.

title: CryptoBridge shutdown announced
description: CryptoBridge announced it would close because of market conditions and increasing regulation.
notes: Deposits were to close on 2019-12-03.

title: CryptoBridge services terminated
description: CryptoBridge said all services and servers would terminate after 2019-12-15.
notes: HEI uses 2019-12-15 as the terminal marker.

title: Coinroom entered the KNF warning list
description: Coinroom was added to the Polish KNF public warning list over suspected unlicensed payment-services activity.
notes: Important regulatory precursor, but not the final terminal event.

title: Coinroom shut down
description: Users reported receiving account-termination notices and Coinroom ceased operating in early April 2019.
notes: Cause remains conservative because later reporting mixes regulatory and fund-loss frames.

title: Withdrawals began failing
description: Users reported that Bitsane withdrawals began failing in May 2019, with support citing temporary technical reasons.
notes: Start-of-failure marker uses month-level dating from aligned reporting.

title: Website and social accounts disappeared
description: By 2019-06-17, the Bitsane website and social media accounts had been deleted and contact emails were bouncing.
notes: Used as the terminal marker for public-ready handling.

title: Cold wallet hack
description: Bter said 7,170 BTC were stolen from its cold wallet and the platform was temporarily shut down.
notes: Major historical incident, but not the final terminal event.

title: Bter users migrated to Gate.io
description: Gate.io announced support for BTER users to move funds to gate.io, reflecting the completed brand transition.
notes: HEI uses 2017-12-31 as the conservative rebrand marker.

title: Abucoins entered the KNF warning list
description: The Polish KNF added Abucoins sp. z o.o. to its public warnings list over suspected unauthorized payment-services activity.
notes: Important regulatory precursor, not the final terminal event.

title: Abucoins closed down
description: By 2019-02-26, Abucoins was publicly marked closed and inactive.
notes: Uses the 2019-02-26 closure marker from public exchange-status sources.

title: Cryptonit marked closed
description: By 2019-04-01, Cryptonit was publicly described as having closed down operations.
notes: Conservative terminal marker from public exchange-status sources.

title: Operations closed
description: A public update stated that Cryptonit had closed down operations, referencing an announcement email to customers.
notes: HEI uses 2019-04-01 as the terminal date.

title: C-CEX became inaccessible
description: By 2019-06-27, public exchange-status sources reported that the website was not accessible.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: C-CEX marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked C-CEX as dead or closed by 2019-06-27.
notes: HEI uses 2019-06-27 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Coinbase announced the transition away from Coinbase Pro
description: Coinbase announced it would replace Coinbase Pro with Coinbase Advanced on Coinbase.
notes: Announcement month used conservatively from official help text.

title: Coinbase Pro discontinued
description: Coinbase documentation states Coinbase Pro was discontinued in December 2023 and replaced by Coinbase Advanced.
notes: HEI uses 2023-12-31 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: BTC38 site failed to load
description: By 2019-06-12, public exchange-status sources reported that the BTC38 website kept loading without completing.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: BTC38 marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked BTC38 as dead by 2019-06-12.
notes: HEI uses 2019-06-12 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Bleutrade announced move to Portugal
description: Bleutrade announced a move to Portugal and closure of its Malta office.
notes: Useful lineage context, not the terminal event.

title: Bleutrade marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources reported the website was inaccessible on 2023-03-23 and marked Bleutrade as dead.
notes: HEI uses 2023-03-23 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: LakeBTC site became inaccessible
description: By 2021-07-15, public exchange-status sources reported that the LakeBTC website was unsuccessful to access.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: LakeBTC marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked LakeBTC as dead on 2021-07-15.
notes: HEI uses 2021-07-15 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Mercatox closure update posted
description: Public exchange-status sources stated that Mercatox had closed down operations and would cease responding to user inquiries on 2025-04-01.
notes: Conservative announcement marker from the public closure update.

title: Mercatox support ended
description: Public closure text stated Mercatox would cease responding to user inquiries on 2025-04-01.
notes: HEI uses 2025-04-01 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: ACX site became inaccessible
description: By 2021-07-15, public exchange-status sources reported that the ACX website was not accessible.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: ACX marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked ACX as dead on 2021-07-15.
notes: HEI uses 2021-07-15 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Cryptox site became inaccessible
description: By 2021-11-11, public exchange-status sources reported that the Cryptox website was not accessible and there had been no prior maintenance notice.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: Cryptox marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked Cryptox as dead on 2021-11-11.
notes: HEI uses 2021-11-11 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: C-Trade platform became unusable
description: By 2023-01-11, public exchange-status sources stated that the C-Trade platform was not possible to use.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: C-Trade marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked C-Trade as dead on 2023-01-11 after the exchange posted a notice about building a brand new platform.
notes: HEI uses 2023-01-11 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: NIX-E showed terminal warning signs
description: By 2019-07-18, public exchange-status sources said NIX-E no longer reported volume to major sites, had an invalid site certificate, and appeared to have stale trading activity.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: NIX-E marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked NIX-E as dead by 2019-07-18.
notes: HEI uses 2019-07-18 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: ZT.com site became inaccessible
description: By 2024-09-18, public exchange-status sources reported that the zt.com website was unsuccessful to access and there had been no prior maintenance or migration notice.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: ZT.com Exchange marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked ZT.com Exchange as dead by 2024-09-18, and CoinMarketCap later showed withdrawals and trading suspended.
notes: HEI uses 2024-09-18 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: AAX suspended withdrawals and operations
description: AAX suspended activity including withdrawals amid a delayed system upgrade and partner failure.
notes: Initial terminal arc marker.

title: AAX marked dead
description: By 2023-01-11, public exchange-status sources said the website was inaccessible and marked AAX as dead.
notes: HEI uses 2023-01-11 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: InstaSwap site became inaccessible
description: By 2024-09-18, public exchange-status sources reported that the InstaSwap website was unsuccessful to access and there had been no prior maintenance or migration notice.
notes: Start of conservative terminal handling.

title: InstaSwap marked dead
description: Public exchange-status sources marked InstaSwap as dead by 2024-09-18.
notes: HEI uses 2024-09-18 as the conservative terminal marker.

title: Uniswap was publicly launched on Ethereum mainnet
description: Uniswap was publicly announced and deployed to Ethereum mainnet on 2018-11-02.
notes: Entity-level launch marker for the protocol family.

title: Uniswap v2 mainnet launch
description: Uniswap v2, the second major protocol version, was deployed to Ethereum mainnet on 2020-05-18.
notes: Major protocol-version milestone under the same umbrella entity.

title: Uniswap v3 mainnet launch
description: Uniswap v3, the third major protocol version, was deployed to Ethereum mainnet on 2021-05-05.
notes: Major protocol-version milestone under the same umbrella entity.

title: SushiSwap launched in August 2020
description: Sushi launched in August 2020 as a decentralized exchange protocol, with official materials describing the project as created in late August 2020.
notes: Month-level conservative launch marker.

title: Sushi announced multichain suite expansion
description: Sushi announced the launch of its suite of DeFi products across major chains, reinforcing its multichain operating model.
notes: Meaningful expansion milestone for the umbrella DEX entity.

title: Sushi introduced Route Processor 6
description: Sushi introduced RP6 as the latest upgrade to its routing stack, showing continued protocol development and execution-layer improvement.
notes: Development-signal milestone retained under event_type=other.

title: PancakeSwap launched in 2020
description: Official PancakeSwap materials describe the protocol as launched in 2020.
notes: Year-level conservative launch marker.

title: PancakeSwap v3 announced in April 2023
description: Official PancakeSwap documentation states that V3 for Exchange and Farms was announced in April 2023.
notes: Month-level conservative marker based on official docs wording.

title: PancakeSwap introduced Infinity
description: PancakeSwap introduced Infinity as a major protocol evolution with a more modular architecture.
notes: Development-signal milestone retained under event_type=other.
